headline: Large Energy User Grid Cost Shifting
short_summary: This bill aims to shift certain electricity costs, such as capacity procurement and distribution infrastructure investments, from residential customers to large energy users like data centers, potentially lowering residential energy bills.
status: Last Action: Governor's recommendation received by House on 2026-04-13
supporters: Senator L. Louise Lucas, residential customers.
opponents: Large energy users, data center operators.
news_headline: Virginia Bills Shift Energy Costs to Data Centers.
news_summary: The 2026 Virginia legislative session focused heavily on regulating the data center industry, with HB 1393 (and its companion SB 253) allowing regulators to shift more energy-related expenses onto data centers. This measure aims to reduce residential customers' energy costs by having large users pay a fairer share.

headline: Data Center Water Consumption Reporting
short_summary: This bill requires water users supplying data centers to report the total volume of potable and reclaimed water provided to the State Water Control Board, increasing transparency on water usage.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 896 (Effective 1/1/2027) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Senator Kannan Srinivasan, communities, environmental groups.
news_headline: Virginia Bill Mandates Data Center Water Use Reporting.
news_summary: Senate Bill 553 addresses water use transparency by requiring utilities to report how much water data centers consume. This aims to give communities clearer insight into local resource use, as such data has often been kept private.

headline: Data Center Siting and Noise Limits
short_summary: This bill requires a site assessment for new high energy use facilities, including a sound profile on nearby residential units and schools, and allows localities to examine impacts on water, agriculture, parks, historic sites, and forestland before approving siting applications.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 567 (Effective 7/1/2026) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Delegate Joshua Thomas, Delegate Sam Rasoul, concerned communities.
news_headline: Virginia Bill Establishes Data Center Siting Requirements.
news_summary: HB 153 introduces new siting requirements for large data centers, mandating impact assessments on agriculture, parks, and historical sites before rezoning approval. Lawmakers view this as a foundation for stronger future regulations on where data centers can be built and how they affect surrounding communities.
